approvalTitle: Day View Keyboard Navigation ŌĆö Tasks Document
content:
# Tasks Document: Day View Keyboard Navigation

## Phase 1: Data Model & Infrastructure

- [x] 1. Create `MCalDayKeyBindings` class
  - File: `lib/src/models/mcal_day_key_bindings.dart` (NEW)
  - Create `MCalDayKeyBindings` immutable class with `const` default constructor. 24 named `List<MCalKeyActivator>` properties across 4 modes: Navigation Mode (8: `enterEventMode`, `home`, `end`, `pageUp`, `pageDown`, `createEvent`, `jumpToAllDay`, `jumpToTimeGrid`), Event Mode (8: `cycleForward`, `cycleBackward`, `activate`, `delete`, `enterMoveMode`, `enterResizeMode`, `exitEventMode`, `convertEventType`), Move Mode (3: `confirmMove`, `cancelMove`, `switchToResize`), Resize Mode (5: `switchToStartEdge`, `switchToEndEdge`, `confirmResize`, `switchToMove`, `cancelResize`). Default values match the Month View where applicable plus Day View-specific additions. Implement `copyWith` for selective overrides. Import `MCalKeyActivator` from `mcal_month_key_bindings.dart`.
  - Purpose: Data model for Day View configurable key bindings
  - _Leverage: `MCalMonthKeyBindings` in `lib/src/models/mcal_month_key_bindings.dart` ŌĆö follow identical patterns for constructor, field declarations, `copyWith`, `==`, `hashCode`, `toString`. Reuse `MCalKeyActivator` from the same file._
  - _Requirements: 6.1, 6.2, 6.3, 6.6, 6.7, 6.8_
  - _Prompt: Role: Dart Developer specializing in immutable data models | Task: Create `MCalDayKeyBindings` in a new file `lib/src/models/mcal_day_key_bindings.dart` as specified in the design doc `.spec-workflow/specs/day-view-keyboard-navigation/design.md` section "Component 1: MCalDayKeyBindings". Import `MCalKeyActivator` from `package:multi_calendar/src/models/mcal_month_key_bindings.dart` and `LogicalKeyboardKey` from `package:flutter/services.dart`. Create 24 named `List<MCalKeyActivator>` properties with `const` defaults exactly as listed in the design. The 3 Day View-specific additions vs Month View are: `jumpToAllDay` (default: A), `jumpToTimeGrid` (default: T), `convertEventType` (default: X). Implement `copyWith` returning a new instance with overridden properties (nullable parameters, use `??` fallback). Implement `==` comparing all 24 fields, `hashCode` using `Object.hashAll`, and `toString` for debugging. Add comprehensive dartdoc for the class and each property group. Arrow keys are NOT included ŌĆö they are hardcoded for directional navigation. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify existing files. Do NOT export from `multi_calendar.dart` yet (Task 2). Both class must have `const` constructor. Follow the exact structure of `MCalMonthKeyBindings` for consistency. | Success: `dart analyze lib/src/models/mcal_day_key_bindings.dart` clean, `const MCalDayKeyBindings()` compiles, all 24 properties have correct default values._

- [x] 2. Export `MCalDayKeyBindings` from `multi_calendar.dart`
  - File: `lib/multi_calendar.dart` (modify existing)
  - Add `export 'src/models/mcal_day_key_bindings.dart';` in alphabetical order with other model exports.
  - Purpose: Make Day View key bindings class part of the public API
  - _Leverage: Existing export pattern in `lib/multi_calendar.dart` ŌĆö see how `mcal_month_key_bindings.dart` is exported_
  - _Requirements: 6.1 (public API availability), NFR Code Architecture_
  - _Prompt: Role: Dart Developer | Task: Add `export 'src/models/mcal_day_key_bindings.dart';` to `lib/multi_calendar.dart`, in alphabetical order with the other model exports. Look at how `mcal_month_key_bindings.dart` is already exported and follow the same pattern. | Restrictions: Do NOT remove existing exports. Only add one line. | Success: `dart analyze` clean, `MCalDayKeyBindings` importable via `package:multi_calendar/multi_calendar.dart`._

- [x] 3. Add focused slot theme properties to `MCalDayThemeData`
  - File: `lib/src/styles/mcal_day_theme_data.dart` (modify existing)
  - Add two new optional properties for Navigation Mode's focused slot visual indicator: `focusedSlotBackgroundColor` (`Color?`) and `focusedSlotDecoration` (`BoxDecoration?`). These allow consumers to customize how the focused time slot appears in Navigation Mode. Add them to the constructor, `copyWith`, `lerp`, `==`, and `hashCode`.
  - Purpose: Theme support for focused slot visual indicator
  - _Leverage: Existing theme properties in `MCalDayThemeData` (e.g., `allDayTileWidth`, `allDayTileHeight`) ŌĆö follow identical pattern for adding new properties_
  - _Requirements: 2.10 (focused slot visually indicated, configurable through MCalDayThemeData)_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer | Task: Add two new optional properties to `MCalDayThemeData` in `lib/src/styles/mcal_day_theme_data.dart`: `final Color? focusedSlotBackgroundColor;` and `final BoxDecoration? focusedSlotDecoration;`. The `focusedSlotBackgroundColor` provides a simple background color for the focused time slot. The `focusedSlotDecoration` provides full control via `BoxDecoration` (border, gradient, etc.) and takes precedence over `focusedSlotBackgroundColor` when both are set. Add both to the constructor as optional named parameters, to `copyWith` with nullable parameters and `??` fallback, to `lerp` (lerp the color, null the decoration since BoxDecoration.lerp exists but is complex), to `==` and `hashCode`. Add dartdoc explaining these are used in Navigation Mode to indicate the focused time slot. | Restrictions: Do NOT remove or modify existing properties. Follow the exact pattern used for `allDayTileWidth` and similar recent additions. | Success: `dart analyze` clean, `MCalDayThemeData(focusedSlotBackgroundColor: Colors.blue.withOpacity(0.1))` compiles._

- [x] 4. Add package-level localization strings for keyboard announcements
  - File: `lib/l10n/app_en.arb` (and `app_ar.arb`, `app_es.arb`, `app_fr.arb`, `app_he.arb`)
  - Add 17 new announcement strings for Day View keyboard navigation to all 5 package-level ARB files. Follow the Month View's `announcement*` naming pattern. Strings: `announcementDayNavigationMode`, `announcementDayEventMode`, `announcementDayEventCycled`, `announcementDayEventSelected`, `announcementDayMoveMode`, `announcementDayResizeMode`, `announcementDayMoveCancelled`, `announcementDayResizeCancelled`, `announcementDayMovingEvent`, `announcementDayResizingEvent`, `announcementDayEventMoved`, `announcementDayEventResized`, `announcementDayAllDaySection`, `announcementDayTimeGrid`, `announcementDayMoveInvalidTarget`, `announcementDayEventDeleted`, `announcementDayEventConversionRequested`. After adding to all ARB files, run `flutter pub get` to regenerate `MCalLocalizations`.
  - Purpose: Localized screen reader announcements for all keyboard mode transitions
  - _Leverage: Existing announcement strings in `lib/l10n/app_en.arb` (e.g., `announcementEventSelected`, `announcementResizeModeEntered`) ŌĆö follow identical naming and metadata patterns. Check `lib/l10n/mcal_localizations.dart` to see generated accessor pattern._
  - _Requirements: NFR Accessibility (localized announcements in all 5 ARB files), 1.6, 3.8, 3.9_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer with i18n expertise | Task: Add 17 new localization strings to all 5 package-level ARB files (`lib/l10n/app_en.arb`, `app_ar.arb`, `app_es.arb`, `app_fr.arb`, `app_he.arb`) as specified in the design doc section "Component 3: Localization additions". For each string, add the key-value pair AND an `@key` metadata entry with `description` and `placeholders` (where applicable). Use ICU message format for parameterized strings ŌĆö e.g., `"announcementDayEventCycled": "{title}, {position} of {total}"` with `@announcementDayEventCycled: { "placeholders": { "title": {}, "position": {}, "total": {} } }`. Translate all strings to the appropriate language for each ARB file (English, Arabic, Spanish, French, Hebrew). After adding all strings, run `flutter pub get` from the project root to regenerate `MCalLocalizations`. Verify the generated `lib/l10n/mcal_localizations.dart` includes the new accessor methods. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify existing strings. Do NOT modify example app ARB files (those are Task 13). Place new entries after existing announcement strings for consistency. Ensure proper JSON syntax. | Success: `flutter pub get` succeeds, `MCalLocalizations` class has new accessors like `announcementDayNavigationMode`, `announcementDayEventCycled(title, position, total)`, etc._

## Phase 2: API Migration & Legacy Removal

- [x] 5. Remove legacy shortcuts system, update callback signatures, add new callbacks and parameters
  - File: `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart` (modify existing)
  - File: `lib/multi_calendar.dart` (modify existing)
  - **Remove from `mcal_day_view.dart`:**
    - `MCalDayViewCreateEventIntent` class (line ~36)
    - `MCalDayViewDeleteEventIntent` class (line ~42)
    - `MCalDayViewEditEventIntent` class (line ~48)
    - `MCalDayViewKeyboardMoveIntent` class (line ~54)
    - `MCalDayViewKeyboardResizeIntent` class (line ~60)
    - `keyboardShortcuts` parameter (constructor line ~219, field line ~710)
    - `onEditEventRequested` parameter (constructor line ~282, field line ~1234)
    - `_buildDefaultShortcuts()` method (line ~5755)
    - `_buildShortcutsMap()` method (line ~5792)
    - `_buildActionsMap()` method (line ~5803)
    - `Shortcuts` and `Actions` widget wrappers in `build()` (line ~6268) ŌĆö replace with direct child
  - **Update callback signatures:**
    - `onCreateEventRequested`: from `VoidCallback?` to `FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, DateTime)?` ŌĆö update constructor, field, dartdoc. Note in dartdoc that the return value is ignored per Requirement 8 AC 6.
    - `onDeleteEventRequested`: from `void Function(MCalCalendarEvent)?` to `FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, MCalEventTapDetails)?` ŌĆö update constructor, field, dartdoc
  - **Add new:**
    - `onEventTypeConversionRequested` parameter: `FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, MCalCalendarEvent, bool toAllDay, DateTime? suggestedStartTime)?`
    - `keyBindings` parameter: `MCalDayKeyBindings?`
  - **Update `multi_calendar.dart`:** Remove `MCalDayViewCreateEventIntent`, `MCalDayViewDeleteEventIntent`, `MCalDayViewEditEventIntent` from the `show` clause of the `mcal_day_view.dart` export. (`MCalDayViewKeyboardMoveIntent` and `MCalDayViewKeyboardResizeIntent` are not exported currently ŌĆö verify.)
  - Purpose: Clean API surface ŌĆö remove legacy, align callback signatures with Month View, add Day View-specific callbacks
  - _Leverage: Month View's callback signatures in `mcal_month_view.dart` for `onCreateEventRequested` and `onDeleteEventRequested` patterns. `MCalEventTapDetails` for the delete callback._
  - _Requirements: 7.1, 7.7, 7.8, 8.2, 8.5, 9.1, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 6.4_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer | Task: Perform the API migration in `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart` as specified in this task. This is a coordinated removal + update: (1) DELETE the 5 Intent classes at the top of the file (~lines 26ŌĆō77). (2) DELETE the `keyboardShortcuts` parameter from the constructor (~line 219) and its field declaration (~line 710) and all dartdoc. (3) DELETE the `onEditEventRequested` parameter from the constructor (~line 282) and its field declaration (~line 1234) and all dartdoc. (4) DELETE `_buildDefaultShortcuts()` (~line 5755), `_buildShortcutsMap()` (~line 5792), and `_buildActionsMap()` (~line 5803). (5) In `build()`, find the `Shortcuts(shortcuts: _buildShortcutsMap(), child: Actions(actions: _buildActionsMap(), child: focusContent))` block (~line 6268) and replace it with just `focusContent` ŌĆö remove the `Shortcuts` and `Actions` wrappers entirely. (6) UPDATE `onCreateEventRequested` field type from `VoidCallback?` to `FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, DateTime)?` with updated dartdoc noting the return value is ignored. (7) UPDATE `onDeleteEventRequested` field type from `void Function(MCalCalendarEvent)?` to `FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, MCalEventTapDetails)?` with updated dartdoc. (8) ADD `final FutureOr<bool> Function(BuildContext, MCalCalendarEvent, bool toAllDay, DateTime? suggestedStartTime)? onEventTypeConversionRequested;` with dartdoc. (9) ADD `final MCalDayKeyBindings? keyBindings;` with dartdoc. (10) In `lib/multi_calendar.dart`, update the `mcal_day_view.dart` export's `show` clause: remove `MCalDayViewCreateEventIntent`, `MCalDayViewDeleteEventIntent`, `MCalDayViewEditEventIntent`. Import `dart:async` for `FutureOr` if not already imported. | Restrictions: Do NOT implement new keyboard handlers yet (that's Phase 3). The existing `_handleKeyEvent`, `_handleKeyboardMoveKey`, `_handleKeyboardResizeKey` remain unchanged for now ŌĆö they will be refactored in Tasks 6ŌĆō10. The existing `_enterKeyboardMoveMode` and `_enterKeyboardResizeMode` methods also remain unchanged. Do NOT change any existing usages of `onCreateEventRequested` or `onDeleteEventRequested` in the current handlers ŌĆö those will be updated in Phase 3. If removing the `_buildActionsMap()` method causes compile errors in `_enterKeyboardMoveMode`/`_enterKeyboardResizeMode` calls, leave those methods in place (they are called from the handlers, not from Actions). | Success: `dart analyze` clean. `MCalDayViewCreateEventIntent` no longer exists. `MCalDayView` no longer has `keyboardShortcuts` or `onEditEventRequested` parameters. New callback parameters compile correctly._

## Phase 3: State Machine Implementation

- [x] 6. Add state fields, refactor `_handleKeyEvent` router, add helper methods
  - File: `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart` (modify existing)
  - **Add state fields** to `_MCalDayViewState`: `bool _isKeyboardEventMode = false`, `int? _focusedSlotIndex` (null = all-day section focused; starts as `null` which means the all-day section has initial focus when the calendar first receives keyboard focus, satisfying Req 1.2 and 2.2), `int? _lastTimeGridSlotIndex`, `bool _isKeyboardOverflowFocused = false`, `int _keyboardEventIndex = 0`.
  - **Add computed property**: `MCalDayKeyBindings get _keyBindings => widget.keyBindings ?? const MCalDayKeyBindings();`
  - **Add helper methods**:
    - `bool _matchesAny(List<MCalKeyActivator> activators, LogicalKeyboardKey key)` ŌĆö extracts modifier state from `HardwareKeyboard.instance`, calls `activator.matches()` on each
    - `void _exitAllKeyboardModes()` ŌĆö resets `_isKeyboardEventMode`, `_isKeyboardMoveMode`, `_isKeyboardResizeMode`, `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused`, `_focusedEvent`, `_keyboardMoveEvent`, etc. to defaults, calls `setState`
    - `void _handleDeleteResult(FutureOr<bool> result)` ŌĆö mirrors Month View's pattern: sync `true` calls `_exitAllKeyboardModes`, async `true` does same after checking `mounted`
    - `void _handleConversionResult(FutureOr<bool> result)` ŌĆö same pattern as `_handleDeleteResult`
  - **Refactor `_handleKeyEvent`**: Replace the current body with the four-mode router as shown in the design. The Escape handling at the top remains (works even with `enableKeyboardNavigation == false`). After the `enableKeyboardNavigation` check, dispatch to `_handleKeyboardResizeModeKey` ŌåÆ `_handleKeyboardMoveModeKey` ŌåÆ `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey` ŌåÆ `_handleNavigationModeKey`. Note: The existing methods `_handleKeyboardResizeKey` and `_handleKeyboardMoveKey` have not been renamed yet at this point ŌĆö use their current names in the router for now. Tasks 9 and 10 will rename them to `_handleKeyboardMoveModeKey` and `_handleKeyboardResizeModeKey` respectively; update the router calls at that time. Create stub methods for `_handleNavigationModeKey` and `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey` that return `KeyEventResult.ignored` (implemented in Tasks 7ŌĆō8).
  - Purpose: Foundation for the four-mode state machine with shared infrastructure
  - _Leverage: Month View's `_matchesAny`, `_handleDeleteResult`, `_exitKeyboardMoveMode` patterns in `mcal_month_view.dart`. Existing Day View `_handleKeyEvent` at line 2368._
  - _Requirements: 1.1, 1.2, 1.7, 1.8, 6.4, 6.5_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer with expertise in key event handling | Task: Add state fields and infrastructure to `_MCalDayViewState` in `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart`. (1) Add state fields near the existing keyboard fields (~line 1544): `bool _isKeyboardEventMode = false;`, `int? _focusedSlotIndex;` (starts as `null`, meaning the all-day section is focused by default when the calendar first receives keyboard focus ŌĆö this satisfies Req 1.2 "WHEN the calendar first receives focus THEN it SHALL be in Navigation Mode" and Req 2.2 about the all-day section being a focusable slot), `int? _lastTimeGridSlotIndex;`, `bool _isKeyboardOverflowFocused = false;`, `int _keyboardEventIndex = 0;`. (2) Add computed property: `MCalDayKeyBindings get _keyBindings => widget.keyBindings ?? const MCalDayKeyBindings();`. (3) Add `_matchesAny` method matching the design doc exactly ŌĆö read modifier state from `HardwareKeyboard.instance`. (4) Add `_exitAllKeyboardModes()` that resets ALL keyboard state: `_isKeyboardEventMode = false`, `_isKeyboardMoveMode = false`, `_isKeyboardResizeMode = false`, `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused = false`, `_focusedEvent = null`, `_keyboardMoveEvent = null`, `_keyboardMoveOriginalStart = null`, `_keyboardMoveOriginalEnd = null`, `_keyboardMoveProposedStart = null`, `_keyboardMoveProposedEnd = null`, `_keyboardResizeEdge = null`, `_keyboardResizeEdgeOffset = 0.0`, `_keyboardEventIndex = 0`, then `setState(() {})`. (5) Add `_handleDeleteResult` and `_handleConversionResult` following the Month View's pattern ŌĆö if `result is Future<bool>`, call `.then((value) { if (mounted && value) _exitAllKeyboardModes(); })`, else if `result == true`, call `_exitAllKeyboardModes()`. (6) Refactor `_handleKeyEvent`: keep the existing Escape block at the top (including its priority order: resize > move > drag). After `if (!widget.enableKeyboardNavigation) return KeyEventResult.ignored;`, add mode dispatch: `if (_isKeyboardResizeMode) return _handleKeyboardResizeKey(event);` ŌåÆ `if (_isKeyboardMoveMode) return _handleKeyboardMoveKey(event);` ŌåÆ `if (_isKeyboardEventMode) return _handleKeyboardEventModeKey(event);` ŌåÆ `return _handleNavigationModeKey(event);`. Note: use the existing method names `_handleKeyboardResizeKey` and `_handleKeyboardMoveKey` here ŌĆö they will be renamed to `_handleKeyboardResizeModeKey` and `_handleKeyboardMoveModeKey` in Tasks 9 and 10 respectively, and the router calls should be updated at that time. Create placeholder `_handleNavigationModeKey(KeyEvent event)` and `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey(KeyEvent event)` that return `KeyEventResult.ignored`. Move existing scroll navigation code from `_handleKeyEvent` into `_handleNavigationModeKey` temporarily so the existing behavior is preserved until Task 7 replaces it. | Restrictions: Do NOT change or rename the existing `_handleKeyboardMoveKey` or `_handleKeyboardResizeKey` methods yet (Tasks 9ŌĆō10 handle that). Preserve existing Escape handling exactly. Import `MCalDayKeyBindings` from the model file. | Success: `dart analyze` clean, existing keyboard behavior unchanged (move/resize modes still work), the new mode dispatch router is in place._

- [x] 7. Implement Navigation Mode handler (`_handleNavigationModeKey`)
  - File: `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart` (modify existing)
  - Replace the placeholder `_handleNavigationModeKey` with the full implementation. Handle: ŌåæŌåō (move `_focusedSlotIndex` up/down by one, with transition to/from all-day section at `null`), Home (set `_focusedSlotIndex = 0`, never all-day), End (set to last slot index), PageUp/PageDown (change display date via controller), Enter/Space (transition to Event Mode if day has events), N (call `onCreateEventRequested` with focused time ŌĆö ignore return value per Req 8 AC 6), A (jump to all-day: `_focusedSlotIndex = null`), T (jump to time grid: restore `_lastTimeGridSlotIndex` or nearest-to-now slot). ŌåÉŌåÆ return `KeyEventResult.ignored`. Auto-scroll to reveal focused slot when it moves outside visible area. Announce section transitions (A/T) via `SemanticsService.sendAnnouncement` using the localization strings from Task 4.
  - Purpose: Full Navigation Mode keyboard handling
  - _Leverage: Existing scroll navigation code in `_handleKeyEvent` (arrow up/down, page up/down, home/end). `timeToOffset` for auto-scroll calculations. `_matchesAny` and `_keyBindings` from Task 6._
  - _Requirements: 2.1ŌĆō2.13, 8.1, 8.3, 8.4, 8.6_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer | Task: Implement `_handleNavigationModeKey(KeyEvent event)` in `_MCalDayViewState`. The method receives a `KeyEvent` and returns `KeyEventResult`. Extract `final key = event.logicalKey;`. Handle these in order: (1) Ōåæ arrow: if `_focusedSlotIndex == 0` set to `null` (all-day), else if `_focusedSlotIndex == null` do nothing (already at top), else decrement by 1. Calculate total slots as `((widget.endHour - widget.startHour) * 60) ~/ (widget.timeSlotDuration?.inMinutes ?? 15)`. Clamp index to 0..totalSlots-1. Auto-scroll via `_scrollToSlot(_focusedSlotIndex)` helper. Announce if transitioning to all-day. Return `handled`. (2) Ōåō arrow: if `_focusedSlotIndex == null` set to 0 (first time slot), else increment by 1, clamp. Auto-scroll. Announce if transitioning from all-day. Return `handled`. (3) Home: `_focusedSlotIndex = 0` (first time slot, never all-day). Auto-scroll. Return `handled`. (4) End: `_focusedSlotIndex = totalSlots - 1`. Auto-scroll. Return `handled`. (5) PageUp: navigate to previous day via `controller.setDisplayDate(addDays(controller.displayDate, -1))`. Return `handled`. (6) PageDown: navigate to next day similarly. Return `handled`. (7) Enter/Space via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.enterEventMode, key)`: check if current day has events (`_allDayEvents.isNotEmpty || _timedEvents.isNotEmpty`). If yes, set `_isKeyboardEventMode = true`, `_keyboardEventIndex = 0`, set `_focusedEvent` to first event in display order (all-day first, then timed), announce "Event mode". If no events, do nothing. Return `handled`. (8) N via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.createEvent, key)`: compute focused time ŌĆö if `_focusedSlotIndex == null`, use display date at midnight; else compute `DateTime(y, m, d, startHour, _focusedSlotIndex * slotMinutes)`. Call `widget.onCreateEventRequested?.call(context, focusedTime)` ŌĆö if result is `Future`, call `.then((_) {})` to discard. Return `handled`. (9) A via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.jumpToAllDay, key)`: set `_focusedSlotIndex = null`, announce "All-day section" using l10n. Return `handled`. (10) T via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.jumpToTimeGrid, key)`: set `_focusedSlotIndex = _lastTimeGridSlotIndex ?? _nearestSlotToNow()`, save in `_lastTimeGridSlotIndex`, auto-scroll, announce "Time grid, {time}" using l10n. Return `handled`. (11) ŌåÉ ŌåÆ arrows: return `KeyEventResult.ignored`. (12) Default: return `KeyEventResult.ignored`. Create helper `_scrollToSlot(int? slotIndex)` that converts slot index to pixel offset using slot height and scrolls the time grid's `ScrollController`. Create helper `int _nearestSlotToNow()` that calculates which slot index corresponds to the current time. Wrap all state changes in `setState(() {})`. Use DST-safe arithmetic from `date_utils.dart` for PageUp/PageDown. | Restrictions: Do NOT modify other mode handlers. Use `_matchesAny` for all configurable keys. Arrow keys remain hardcoded. | Success: `dart analyze` clean. ŌåæŌåō navigate through time slots and all-day section. Home/End jump to first/last time slot. A/T jump between sections. Enter enters Event Mode. N calls create callback._

- [x] 8. Implement Event Mode handler (`_handleKeyboardEventModeKey`)
  - File: `lib/src/widgets/mcal_day_view.dart` (modify existing)
  - Replace the placeholder `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey` with the full implementation. Build a display-order event list: visible all-day events (capped by `_allDayVisibleCount`) + overflow indicator (if overflow exists) + all timed events. Handle: Tab/Ōåō cycle forward through the list (wrap around), Shift+Tab/Ōåæ cycle backward. When the overflow indicator is focused, set `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused = true` and `_focusedEvent = null`. Enter/Space: if `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused`, fire `widget.onOverflowTap` and exit all modes; else fire `onEventTap` with the selected event and exit all modes. D/Delete/Backspace: if not overflow-focused, call `onDeleteEventRequested` with `MCalEventTapDetails`, process result via `_handleDeleteResult`. X: if not overflow-focused, call `onEventTypeConversionRequested` with the event, computed `toAllDay` and `suggestedStartTime`, process result via `_handleConversionResult`. M: if not overflow-focused, call `_enterKeyboardMoveMode()`. R: if not overflow-focused, call `_enterKeyboardResizeMode()` **only if resize is enabled** (check `widget.enableDragToResize` / `_resolveDragToResize()`); if resize is disabled, absorb R with no action (per Requirement 1.5). Escape: exit to Navigation Mode. ŌåÉŌåÆ: absorb. Auto-scroll to reveal selected event. Announce event title and position on each cycle.
  - Purpose: Event cycling with activation, deletion, conversion, and mode transitions
  - _Leverage: Existing `_handleTabNavigation` (line 3014) for cycling logic and `_allDayVisibleCount` awareness. `_enterKeyboardMoveMode` and `_enterKeyboardResizeMode` for mode entry. Month View's `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey` for overflow cycling pattern._
  - _Requirements: 3.1ŌĆō3.14, 7.1ŌĆō7.6, 9.1ŌĆō9.7_
  - _Prompt: Role: Flutter Developer | Task: Implement `_handleKeyboardEventModeKey(KeyEvent event)` in `_MCalDayViewState`. Build a unified display-order list of focusable items: `final visibleAllDay = _allDayVisibleCount != null ? _allDayEvents.take(_allDayVisibleCount!).toList() : _allDayEvents;`, then `final hasOverflow = _allDayVisibleCount != null && _allDayEvents.length > _allDayVisibleCount!;`, then `final allFocusable = [...visibleAllDay, ..._timedEvents];`. The total cyclable count is `allFocusable.length + (hasOverflow ? 1 : 0)` where the overflow indicator sits at index `visibleAllDay.length` in the cycle. Track position via `_keyboardEventIndex`. Handle keys in order: (1) cycleForward/cycleBackward via `_matchesAny`: increment/decrement `_keyboardEventIndex`, wrap around using modulo. If index lands on the overflow position (`visibleAllDay.length` when `hasOverflow`), set `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused = true`, `_focusedEvent = null`. Otherwise set `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused = false`, adjust index for timed events (subtract overflow offset), set `_focusedEvent = allFocusable[adjustedIndex]`. Auto-scroll to reveal the event. Announce via l10n `announcementDayEventCycled(title, position, total)`. Return `handled`. (2) activate via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.activate, key)`: if `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused`, fire `widget.onOverflowTap?.call()`, then `_exitAllKeyboardModes()`. Else construct `MCalEventTapDetails(event: _focusedEvent!, displayDate: controller.displayDate)`, fire `widget.onEventTap?.call(context, details)`, then `_exitAllKeyboardModes()`. Return `handled`. Return `handled`. (4) convertEventType via `_matchesAny(_keyBindings.convertEventType, key)`: if `_isKeyboardOverflowFocused`, return `handled`. (5) enterMoveMode via `_matchesAny`: if not overflow-focused, call `_enterKeyboardMoveMode()`. Return `handled`.
